item.title: cmd_backlog_add missing validate_arg calls for most flags
item.intent: backlog add must validate that flag arguments are non-empty — silently accepting empty values creates malformed backlog items that fail sprint readiness checks without a clear error message
item.description: cmd_backlog_add() calls validate_arg for --step (line 3617) but not for --type, --title, --intent, --desc, --priority, --category, or --depends-on (lines 3592-3600). This means 'backlog add --title' (with no value) silently sets title to an empty string, producing a malformed item. By contrast, cmd_backlog_edit() consistently calls validate_arg for all value-bearing flags. Fix: add validate_arg calls in cmd_backlog_add() to match the pattern already established in cmd_backlog_edit().
steps: In .aishore/aishore, find cmd_backlog_add() and locate the flag handling block (around lines 3592-3617)

For each flag that takes a value (--type, --title, --intent, --desc, --priority, --category, --depends-on), add validate_arg "$1" "${2:-}" || return 1 before the assignment, matching the pattern in cmd_backlog_edit()
Verify that 'backlog add --title' (no value) now prints an error and exits non-zero
Verify that 'backlog add --title "My Item" --priority must' still creates the item correctly
acceptanceCriteria: backlog add --title (with no value) exits with a non-zero status and prints an error
backlog add --intent (with no value) exits with a non-zero status and prints an error
backlog add with all required values still creates items correctly
